_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Maintain incident tracking and solution databases
reference_document_element_description: Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, blocker escalation, and closure verification with evidence.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. Task RC-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-006 (RC, cosine: 0.519), DG-T-007 (DG, cosine: 0.482).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.4/10 (raw 0.337). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [databases, incident, maintain, solution, tracking] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, blocker, closure, compliance, data, escalation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.875, R=0.862). Balanced P/R — the 6-word NICE element and 15-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Maintain incident tracking and solution databases" and "Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, bl" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.534 > STS 0.337 (gap +0.198). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [databases, incident, maintain, solution, tracking]. WIDAI terms: [activities, blocker, closure, compliance, data, escalation].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.198 with low STS 0.337. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
